commit.message:
✨ feat: add --skip-commit flag to bypass git checks

Allows kettle-jem to run in directories not tracked by git.

When --skip-commit is passed:
- The .git directory check in prechecks! is skipped (with a helpful hint
  in the error message if git IS required but absent).
- The dirty-worktree check is bypassed entirely.
- debug_git_status is a no-op (avoids shelling out to git unnecessarily).
- commit_bootstrap_changes! returns immediately without staging or committing.

The binstub --help text is updated to document the new flag.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[copp] Pin ptf_nn_agent.py wget URLs to nnpy-compatible PTF commit (workaround) (#23789)

Pin the `wget` download URLs in `copp_utils.py` for `ptf_nn_agent.py`
and `afpacket.py` to the last nnpy-compatible PTF commit (`9d41838d`),
instead of tracking `master`. This is a workaround to prevent COPP tests
from hanging on testbeds where only `nnpy` is installed.

### Description of PR

Summary:

On Apr 5 2026, p4lang/ptf PR #234 (commit `ebc00f94`) changed
`ptf_nn_agent.py` to import `pynng` instead of `nnpy`. Current SONiC
production PTF container images have `nnpy` installed but NOT `pynng`.
When `copp_utils.py` downloads `ptf_nn_agent.py` from the unversioned
`master` URL, it gets the new pynng-based version, which crashes
immediately on startup. This causes port 10900 to never open, and COPP
tests time out after 600 seconds.

This PR pins all three `wget` call sites in `copp_utils.py` to commit
`9d41838d634c479fc24fac7a527ec5ee2d0ce8eb` (Apr 2 2026 — last
nnpy-compatible state) for both `ptf_nn_agent.py` and `afpacket.py`.

The proper long-term fix is for the PTF container image build to install
`pynng`. This PR is an explicit workaround to unblock COPP tests until
that infrastructure update is available.

### Type of change

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Testbed and Framework(new/improvement)
- [ ] New Test case
    - [ ] Skipped for non-supported platforms
- [ ] Test case improvement

### Back port request
- [ ] 202205
- [ ] 202305
- [ ] 202311
- [ ] 202405
- [ ] 202411
- [ ] 202505
- [x] 202511

### Approach

#### What is the motivation for this PR?
After p4lang/ptf commit `ebc00f94` (Apr 5 2026), `ptf_nn_agent.py`
requires `pynng`. SONiC production PTF containers only have `nnpy`. The
unversioned `master` URL causes a crash at startup, blocking all COPP
tests.

#### How did you do it?
Changed 6 URL occurrences across 3 code paths in `copp_utils.py`
(`_install_nano_bookworm`, `_install_nano` bullseye path,
`_install_nano` buster/py2 path) from `master` to pinned commit hash
`9d41838d634c479fc24fac7a527ec5ee2d0ce8eb` for both `ptf_nn_agent.py`
and `afpacket.py`.

#### How did you verify/test it?
Verified on internal testbed (bjw13-5, DUT bjw-can-7260-11) with 202511
branch — COPP tests pass after pinning. PTF container on the testbed
confirmed to have `nnpy` v0.10.0 installed, `pynng` absent.

passed on T0 and T1
https://elastictest.org/scheduler/testplan/69d824c02e61bab317c442d5
https://elastictest.org/scheduler/testplan/69d824d559cbd7d94a4fe3b4

#### Any platform specific information?
Affects all platforms that run COPP tests with `copp_utils.py` network
namespace (NN) agent setup.

#### Supported testbed topology if it's a new test case?
N/A (bug fix, not a new test case)

### Documentation

N/A

Signed-off-by: Liping Xu <xuliping@microsoft.com>

[CK] Add flash_attn tests (#5329)

## Motivation

Add CI support for running
[flash-attention](https://github.com/ROCm/flash-attention) tests against
CK, similar to existing AITER and PyTorch downstream test pipelines.

## Technical Details

### New: `Dockerfile.fa`
A new Dockerfile that builds a flash-attention test image on top of a
ROCm PyTorch base image. It:
- Sparse-checkouts CK from `rocm-libraries` (or clones directly from
`ROCm/composable_kernel`)
- Clones and builds `flash-attention` with CK as the backend
- Supports configurable `FA_BRANCH`, `CK_FA_BRANCH`, and `GPU_ARCHS`
build args

### Updated: `Jenkinsfile`

**buildDocker refactor:**
- Extracted `buildAndPushDockerImage()` helper that handles both "check
if exists, skip" and "force build, push" logic, eliminating the
duplicated try/catch blocks
- Split monolithic `buildDocker()` into `buildDockerBase()`,
`buildDockerPytorch()`, `buildDockerAiter()`, and new `buildDockerFa()`
- Each downstream docker build now runs unconditionally within its
respective guard (`RUN_PYTORCH_TESTS`, `RUN_AITER_TESTS`,
`RUN_FA_TESTS`)
- Image digests are stored in env vars (`CK_BASE_IMAGE`,
`CK_PYTORCH_IMAGE`, `CK_AITER_IMAGE`, `CK_FA_IMAGE`) for use in
downstream stages

**run_downstream_tests refactor:**
- Merged `run_aiter_tests()` and `run_pytorch_tests()` into a single
generic `run_downstream_tests(conf)` that accepts `image`,
`timeoutHours`, and `execute_cmds`
- Test commands for each downstream target are declared as top-level
lists (`RUN_PYTORCH_TESTS_CMDS`, `RUN_AITER_TESTS_CMDS`,
`RUN_FA_TESTS_CMDS`)

**Pipeline stages:**
- Merged "Run Pytorch Tests" and "Run AITER Tests" into a single "Run
Downstream Tests" parallel stage
- Added two new FA test stages: "Run FA Tests on gfx942" and "Run FA
Tests on gfx950"
- Added new pipeline parameters: `RUN_FA_TESTS`, `fa_base_docker`,
`fa_branch`, `ck_fa_branch`
- `ck_pytorch_branch` and `ck_aiter_branch` now default to the current
branch instead of hardcoded `develop`
- CRON schedule at 13:00 now also triggers `RUN_FA_TESTS=true`

## Test Plan

- [x] Trigger pipeline manually with `RUN_FA_TESTS=true` on gfx942 and
gfx950 nodes
- [x] Verify existing AITER and PyTorch test stages are unaffected
- [x] Verify `buildAndPushDockerImage` correctly skips rebuild when
image already exists (with `BUILD_DOCKER=false`)

## Test Result

<!-- Briefly summarize test outcomes. -->

https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests.

commit.message:
🐛 fix: apply strip_self_from_templating_gemfile to all modular gemfiles

Previously, only templating.gemfile had the host-gem self-reference
stripped. But coverage.gemfile (and potentially other modular gemfiles)
also list specific kettle-rb gems as dependencies — e.g. coverage.gemfile
contains `gem "kettle-soup-cover"`. When kettle-soup-cover runs kettle-jem
on itself, its coverage.gemfile must not declare a dependency on itself.

The fix applies strip_self_from_templating_gemfile to every modular gemfile
(both template_content and post-merge content), while keeping
strip_self_from_templating_local scoped to templating_local.gemfile only
(that method handles the specialized %w[] array / VENDORED_GEMS comment
format unique to that file).

commit.message:
fix(#58): reactive engine init — use file existence not WorkManager state

The one-shot engine init in initializeConversation() failed silently when
the preferred model's WorkManager task was still RUNNING or FAILED at startup
(e.g. E4B worker was mid-download when the process was restarted). In that
case downloadStates.value[preferred] == Downloading/Error, not Downloaded,
so the initialize() call was skipped and the app stuck on Loading indefinitely.

Two fixes:
1. Replace one-shot init with initEngineWhenReady() — a reactive coroutine
   that waits on downloadStates until all required models report Downloaded,
   then initialises the engine. Fires immediately if models are already on disk.
2. Use downloadManager.getModelPath() (file-existence check) for the model path
   rather than the StateFlow value, so models pushed manually via ADB are
   recognised regardless of WorkManager task state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🐛 fix: template all modular gemfiles in preflight

Replace narrow BOOTSTRAP_MODULAR_GEMFILES list + empty-stub fallback
with a full recursive walk of the installed template directory.

Previously, only templating.gemfile and templating_local.gemfile were
written in the pre-bundled phase; every other modular gemfile received
an empty stub. Empty stubs caused `bundle install` to fail with
`cannot load such file -- rubocop/rake_task` (and similar) when the
Rakefile tried to require gems that were absent from the bundle.

New behaviour:
- preflight_merge_modular_gemfiles! globs all *.gemfile.example files
  recursively under the installed template/gemfiles/modular/ tree.
- Each file is token-resolved and merged (or written fresh) into the
  destination repo before bundle install runs.
- The BOOTSTRAP_MODULAR_GEMFILES / BOOTSTRAP_FORCEABLE_MODULAR_GEMFILES
  constants are removed as no longer needed.
- preflight_ensure_eval_gemfile_stubs! and ensure_bootstrap_modular_gemfiles!
  are removed (dead code whose job is now done by the glob-based walk).

Install python3-ijson from Debian repos for route_check performance (#26633)

PR #4294 forced the pure-Python ijson backend in route_check.py to avoid
yajl2_cffi crashes under monit's MemoryDenyWriteExecute=true. However, the
python backend is slow, causing a regression on scaled platforms (21 min for
100K routes on Nokia chassis).

Root cause: sonic-yang-mgmt and sonic-yang-models pin ijson==3.2.3 (exact),
forcing pip to download from PyPI even when Debian's python3-ijson (3.4.0,
with the fast yajl2_c C extension) is installed. The PyPI wheel for Python
3.13 lacks the _yajl2 C extension, and pip's copy at /usr/local/lib/ shadows
the Debian package at /usr/lib/python3/.

Fix:
- Relax ijson==3.2.3 to ijson>=3.2.3 in sonic-yang-mgmt and sonic-yang-models
- Install python3-ijson via apt before pip installs sonic-yang-mgmt, so pip
  sees Debian's 3.4.0 and skips the PyPI download entirely

With this change, ijson auto-detects the yajl2_c backend (24x faster than
the python backend, safe under MemoryDenyWriteExecute).

Fixes: sonic-net/sonic-buildimage#23442

Signed-off-by: Deepak Singhal <deepsinghal@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
fix(#56): download worker saves to external storage to match isDownloaded() check

ModelDownloadWorker was saving to context.filesDir (internal storage) but
KernelModel.localFile() and isDownloaded() resolve against
context.getExternalFilesDir("models") (scoped external storage).

This mismatch meant downloaded models were never detected as present,
causing the app to re-download on every launch and show "Loading model..."
indefinitely after a completed download.

Fix: use getExternalFilesDir("models") with filesDir fallback, matching
KernelModel.localFile() exactly.

* fix: prevent flaky ResourceSampling test from CPU measurement spikes (#192)

When SampleAllPluginsAsync is called back-to-back (as tests do),
wallClockMs between calls is near-zero, producing astronomically high
CPU percentages. This can trigger IsAggregateLimitExceeded(), suspending
the plugin before violations reach the warning threshold — causing
PluginResourceLimitEvent to never be published.

Changes:
- Initialize _lastProcessCpuTime and _lastSampleTime in constructor
  (not just ExecuteAsync) so the first sample has a proper baseline
- Add MinSampleIntervalMs (10ms) guard on CPU calculation to prevent
  unrealistic spikes from sub-millisecond elapsed times
- Lower test MaxMemoryMb from 16 to 1 for extra robustness
